label: Reinforcement learning

node.label: Reinforcement learning
node.definition: An agent learns behavior from rewards while interacting with an environment over time.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning is modeled as depending on Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Model-based reinforcement learning, Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning, Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives.
examples: atlas downstream link to Model-based reinforcement learning
atlas downstream link to Imitation & inverse reinforcement learning
atlas downstream link to Bellman, temporal-difference & value objectives
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Game theory & decision theory, Dynamical systems & control theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Game theory & decision theory because Game theory & decision theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Dynamical systems & control theory because Dynamical systems & control theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states because Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Labels, targets, rewards & preferences because Labels, targets, rewards & preferences is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Reinforcement learning has a `uses` dependency on Sequences, events & time series because Sequences, events & time series is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
